div.h1: Why realloc() Cannot Work for Memory Pools
div.h2: Existing pointers become invalid when memory moves

g.text: AFTER: realloc() moved memory → DANGLING POINTERS

div.p: realloc() is oblivious to the pointers your application holds.
When it moves memory to find a contiguous larger block:

ol.li: The OS maps the physical pages to a new virtual address (e.g., 0x5000).
The old virtual address (0x1000) is unmapped or marked free.
Your variables struct_a.data, cache->buffer, etc., still contain the old address values.

div.p: Result: High-frequency, unpredictable crashes or silent data corruption. Design Rule: For memory pools, use Chunk-Chaining (Singly Linked Lists of fixed-size blocks) instead of resizing contiguous buffers.
Memory returned to OS No longer belongs to Pool
